codeReview.summary: The feature established the new shared lifecycle/reference DTOs and audit provenance chain, but one of the core lifecycle helpers still collapses commercial-order state into an accounting-pending state before any canonical posting trigger exists. That undercuts the milestone's goal of separating workflow state from accounting eligibility.

issues.description: salesOrderLifecycle marks any sales order with a non-blank workflow status as accounting `PENDING` even when the order has neither `fulfillmentInvoiceId` nor `salesJournalEntryId`. This means a commercial source order surfaced in linked references can appear accounting-eligible before dispatch/invoice truth exists, which conflicts with the intended workflow-vs-accounting separation for O2C documents.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill still makes frontend-handoff/doc updates mandatory even in missions where higher-priority instructions forbid documentation edits unless the user explicitly requested them.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend-worker/SKILL.md Step 5 requires frontend-handoff updates; this feature's handoff skillFeedback records a justified deviation because the session instructions prohibited doc changes and the packet kept to code/tests only.

summary: Fail. The shared truth contract scaffolding is valuable, but sales-order accounting lifecycle is still overstated as `PENDING` before any accounting trigger exists.
